result.stdout:
USAGE: FSharp.Analyzers.Cli [--help] [--project [<string>...]]
                            [--script [<string>...]]
                            [--analyzers-path [<string>...]]
                            [--property <string>=<string>]
                            [--configuration <string>] [--runtime <string>]
                            [--arch <string>] [--os <string>]
                            [--treat-as-info [<string>...]]
                            [--treat-as-hint [<string>...]]
                            [--treat-as-warning [<string>...]]
                            [--treat-as-error [<string>...]]
                            [--exclude-files [<string>...]]
                            [--include-files [<string>...]]
                            [--exclude-analyzers [<string>...]]
                            [--include-analyzers [<string>...]]
                            [--report <string>] [--fsc-args <string>]
                            [--fsc-args-file <string>] [--code-root <string>]
                            [--verbosity <string>] [--output-format <string>]
                            [--binlog-path <string>]

OPTIONS:

    --project [<string>...]
                          List of paths to your .fsproj file. Cannot be
                          combined with `--fsc-args`.
    --script [<string>...]
                          List of paths to your .fsx file. Supports globs.
                          Cannot be combined with `--fsc-args`.
    --analyzers-path [<string>...]
                          List of path to a folder where your analyzers are
                          located. This will search recursively.
    --property, -p:, -p <string>=<string>
                          A key=value pair of an MSBuild property.
    --configuration, -c <string>
                          The configuration to use, e.g. Debug or Release.
    --runtime, -r <string>
                          The runtime identifier (RID).
    --arch, -a <string>   The target architecture.
    --os <string>         The target operating system.
    --treat-as-info [<string>...]
                          List of analyzer codes that should be treated as
                          severity Info by the tool. Regardless of the original
                          severity.
    --treat-as-hint [<string>...]
                          List of analyzer codes that should be treated as
                          severity Hint by the tool. Regardless of the original
                          severity.
    --treat-as-warning [<string>...]
                          List of analyzer codes that should be treated as
                          severity Warning by the tool. Regardless of the
                          original severity.
    --treat-as-error [<string>...]
                          List of analyzer codes that should be treated as
                          severity Error by the tool. Regardless of the
                          original severity.
    --exclude-files [<string>...]
                          Source files that shouldn't be processed.
    --include-files [<string>...]
                          Source files that should be processed exclusively
                          while all others are ignored. Takes precedence over
                          --exclude-files.
    --exclude-analyzers [<string>...]
                          The names of analyzers that should not be executed.
    --include-analyzers [<string>...]
                          The names of analyzers that should exclusively be
                          executed while all others are ignored. Takes
                          precedence over --exclude-analyzers.
    --report <string>     Write the result messages to a (sarif) report file.
    --fsc-args <string>   Pass in the raw fsc compiler arguments. Cannot be
                          combined with the `--project` flag.
    --fsc-args-file <string>
                          Path to a response (RSP) file containing fsc compiler
                          arguments. Cannot be combined with `--project` or
                          `--fsc-args` flags.
    --code-root <string>  Root of the current code repository, used in the
                          sarif report to construct the relative file path. The
                          current working directory is used by default.
    --verbosity, -v <string>
                          The verbosity level. The available verbosity levels
                          are: n[ormal], d[etailed], diag[nostic].
    --output-format <string>
                          Format in which to write analyzer results to stdout.
                          The available options are: default, github.
    --binlog-path <string>
                          Path to a directory where MSBuild binary logs
                          (binlog) will be written. You can use
                          https://msbuildlog.com/ to view them.
    --help                display this list of options.
